definitions.definition: The share capital of a company which is raised by issuing shares to the public in exchange for cash. Shareholders are entitled to a fixed dividend.
definitions.example: For example, ABC Limited issued 100 equity shares of Rs. 100 each to the public.

definitions.definition: The share capital of a company which is raised by issuing debentures or bonds. The company has to pay a fixed rate of interest.
definitions.example: For example, XYZ Limited issued 50 debentures of Rs. 1,000 each to the public.

definitions.definition: The profit which is not distributed among shareholders but is retained by the company for reinvestment or future needs.
definitions.example: For example, a company made a profit of Rs. 1,00,000 and retained Rs. 50,000.

definitions.definition: The amount of money raised by a company by issuing shares to the public in exchange for cash.
definitions.example: For example, a company raised Rs. 10 lakhs by issuing shares to the public.

definitions.definition: A loan which is taken for a period of more than one year.
definitions.example: For example, a company took a loan of Rs. 50 lakhs from a bank for a period of 5 years.

definitions.definition: A loan which is taken for a period of less than one year.
definitions.example: For example, a company took a loan of Rs. 10 lakhs from a bank for a period of 6 months.

definitions.definition: A portion of the profit which is distributed among shareholders in the form of cash or stock.
definitions.example: For example, a company paid a dividend of 10% on its equity shares.

definitions.definition: A type of debt instrument which is issued by a company to raise long-term funds.
definitions.example: For example, a company issued debentures of Rs. 1,000 each to the public.

definitions.definition: A type of deposit which is made for a fixed period of time and earns a fixed rate of interest.
definitions.example: For example, a company took a fixed deposit of Rs. 50 lakhs from a bank for a period of 2 years.

definitions.definition: A liability which is to be paid within a short period of time, usually less than one year.
definitions.example: For example, a company has to pay a utility bill of Rs. 10,000 within a week.

definitions.definition: A liability which is to be paid after a long period of time, usually more than one year.
definitions.example: For example, a company has to pay a loan of Rs. 50 lakhs in 5 years.

definitions.definition: A set amount which is set aside by a company to meet its future liabilities.
definitions.example: For example, a company sets aside Rs. 10,000 as provision for depreciation.

definitions.definition: A set amount which is set aside by a company to meet its future needs or to provide a cushion against losses.
definitions.example: For example, a company sets aside Rs. 50,000 as general reserve.

definitions.definition: An asset which is expected to be converted into cash or used up within a short period of time, usually less than one year.
definitions.example: For example, a company has a current asset of Rs. 50,000 in the form of cash.

definitions.definition: An asset which is expected to be converted into cash or used up after a long period of time, usually more than one year.
definitions.example: For example, a company has a long-term asset of Rs. 50 lakhs in the form of machinery.

definitions.definition: The money collected from the public by a company through deposits or loans.
definitions.example: For example, a company collected Rs. 10 lakhs from the public through deposits.

definitions.definition: The profit which is not distributed among shareholders but is retained by the company for reinvestment or future needs.
definitions.example: For example, a company made a profit of Rs. 1,00,000 and retained Rs. 50,000.

definitions.definition: The amount which is received by a company when its shares are issued at a price higher than their face value.
definitions.example: For example, a company issued shares at Rs. 100 each, but the face value was Rs. 50.

definitions.definition: A reserve which is created by a company from its retained earnings to pay future dividends.
definitions.example: For example, a company created a reserve of Rs. 50,000 for paying future dividends.
